role: The central address-routing spine: parses ui:// addresses, maintains a locus pub/sub, and dispatches click/programmatic navigation to a single DOM spotlight sink.
capabilities: Parse and validate ui:// addresses with optional @state segment
Stamp data-ui-ref props on addressable elements
Tiny locus pub/sub store with subscribe/get/set
Indicate an address by toggling .ui-indicated class
Resolve ui:// targets via single sink with spotlight glow, smooth scroll, and fail-loud notice
Fetch context bundle for an address
Install capture-phase click listener on data-ui-ref elements
Install pointer bridge for ui:point CustomEvent
substance_why: The file implements a full parser, pub/sub locus store, two event-driven sinks, DOM spotlight/scroll effects, notice errors, and an API bridge with explicit behavioral rules.
interactions_present: Click capture on any [data-ui-ref] element resolves the ui:// address
CustomEvent ui:point resolves the address transiently
Malformed addresses publish a notice via the locus store
Locus subscription lets React/other consumers reflect current address/notice
contextAt fetches backend context for an address
performance_risks: document.querySelector attribute-selector scan on every click and ui:point across the whole DOM
setTimeout per spotlight event plus classList churn
smooth scrollIntoView can fire repeatedly on rapid events
No throttling on rapid ui:point dispatches
Minimal cssEscape may produce invalid selectors
Synchronous iteration over all subscribers on every locus change
reuse_why: The ui:// address concept, single sink, and locus store are mandated architecture, but the DOM effects, event wiring, and CSS escaping need rebuilding.
decomposition_hints: Split address grammar/parser into its own module
Split locus store/state management into its own module
Split DOM effects (indicate, spotlight, scroll) into a renderer module
Split input adapters (click capture, pointer bridge, future voice/gesture) into separate modules
Split context fetch/API bridge into its own module
Split notice/feedback handling into its own module
